page: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 12 vCPUs across 6 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A defining characteristic of this instance is its bundled local storage, consisting of 7500 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. It does not include any GPU accelerators. For connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. This combination of high-capacity local NVMe storage and high-speed networking is designed for data-intensive workloads requiring rapid local read and write operations.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth tests consistently rank in the middle 50% average tier. Database operations, such as Redis SET operations yielding 9.96 million operations per second, and Passmark database tests also demonstrate average tier performance. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 71.38 GB/sec for read operations and 45.05 GB/sec for write operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the i3en.3xlarge offers a high density of local NVMe storage and memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads where local storage capacity is the primary constraint. It is well-suited for NoSQL databases, distributed file systems, transactional databases, and data warehousing applications that require substantial local storage and moderate multi-core processing power.
description: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 12 vCPUs, 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a substantial 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on an x86_64 architecture at 3.1 GHz, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, though its single-core performance is relatively weak. With a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps and no GPU acceleration, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-heavy deployments. It is highly suited for database operations, distributed file systems, and data warehousing workloads that require massive local storage capacity alongside balanced memory resources.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i3en.3xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 12 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and high-capacity storage workloads.

tagline: A storage-optimized virtual server delivering massive local NVMe SSD capacity and balanced memory for data-intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 12 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz
7500 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for NoSQL databases, distributed file systems, and data warehousing
